answer_facts.UG Programs (B.Tech): Computer Science & Engineering (CSE), Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE), Electrical & Electronics Engineering (EEE), Mechanical Engineering (ME), Civil Engineering (CE), Information Technology (IT)
answer_facts.PG Programs: M.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering, Master of Computer Applications (MCA)
answer_facts.Annual Intake: 480 for UG programs, 18 for PG program in CSE
answer_facts.Approval: Approved by AICTE and affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University

answer_facts.Address: LBS College of Engineering, Muliyar, Kasaragod District, Kerala - 671542, India
answer_facts.Location: Situated in Muliyar Panchayath, 12 km from Kasaragod town
answer_facts.Office Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (Monday to Friday), 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM (Saturday)

answer_facts.Facilities: Reading rooms, reference section, digital library, photocopying

answer_facts.Application Mode: Through KEAM (Kerala Engineering Architecture Medical) counselling
answer_facts.Eligibility: 10+2 with minimum 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics
answer_facts.Lateral Entry: Diploma holders can apply for direct second year admission
answer_facts.Important Dates: KEAM application usually opens in January, counselling in June-July
answer_facts.Documents Required: KEAM rank card, 10th & 12th marksheets, transfer certificate, conduct certificate, passport size photos

answer_facts.Other Fees: Caution deposit (refundable): ₹5,000, Library fee: ₹1,500, Exam fee: ₹2,000 per semester
answer_facts.Payment Mode: Online payment available through college portal
answer_facts.Scholarships: Merit scholarships, SC/ST scholarships, EWS scholarships available

answer_facts.Boys Hostel: Separate hostel blocks with capacity for 300 students
answer_facts.Girls Hostel: Secure hostel with capacity for 200 students, known as Shahanas Hostel
answer_facts.Rooms: Single, double, and triple sharing rooms available
answer_facts.Facilities: Wi-Fi, reading room, TV room, indoor games, gym, laundry
answer_facts.Mess: Separate vegetarian and non-vegetarian mess with North & South Indian food
answer_facts.Medical: First aid facility, tie-up with nearby hospitals
answer_facts.Visiting Hours: Parents can visit on weekends with prior permission

answer_facts.Placement Cell: Dedicated Training & Placement Cell with full-time coordinator
answer_facts.Companies Visited: TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Tech Mahindra, Cognizant, Bosch, BYJU'S, etc.
answer_facts.Training Programs: Aptitude training, technical workshops, mock interviews, GD sessions
answer_facts.Internships: Summer internships with stipend in reputed companies
answer_facts.Higher Studies: Guidance for GATE, CAT, GRE, TOEFL exams

answer_facts.Office Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (Monday to Friday), 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM (Saturday)

answer_facts.Library Hours: 8:30 AM to 8:00 PM (Weekdays), 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM (Saturday), 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM (Sunday)
answer_facts.Holidays: Follows Kerala Government and University holidays

answer_facts.Playground: Large playground for cricket, football, volleyball
answer_facts.Indoor Stadium: Multi-purpose indoor stadium for badminton, table tennis, chess
answer_facts.Sports Offered: Cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, badminton, table tennis, chess, carrom
answer_facts.Coaching: Professional coaching available for major sports

answer_facts.Computer Labs: 8 computer labs with 400+ systems, high-speed internet
answer_facts.Department Labs: Separate labs for each engineering department
answer_facts.Special Labs: CAD/CAM lab, DSP lab, VLSI lab, Networking lab
answer_facts.Equipment: Modern equipment as per AICTE and university norms
answer_facts.Software: Licensed software: MATLAB, AutoCAD, ANSYS, Oracle, etc.
answer_facts.Timings: 8:30 AM to 8:00 PM (All days including weekends during project work)
answer_facts.Research Centers: Mechanical Engineering and Computer Science & Engineering recognized as research centres

answer_facts.Full Name: Lal Bahadur Shastri College of Engineering, Kasaragod
answer_facts.Managed by: L B S Centre for Science and Technology, Thiruvananthapuram (Government of Kerala autonomous body)
answer_facts.Initial Programs: Started with 4 B.Tech programs (ME, EEE, CSE, ECE) with 240 seats
answer_facts.Campus Development: Current campus constructed at 52 acres of land given by Plantation Corporation Kerala at Povval
answer_facts.Growth Timeline: IT program added in 2000, Civil Engineering in 2008, PG programs added 2013-15
answer_facts.University Affiliations: Initially under Calicut University (1993-1996), then Kannur University (1996-2015), now APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (from 2015)
answer_facts.Infrastructure Growth: Started with 20,000 sq.ft., now expanded to 2.5 lakh sq.ft. with separate department buildings
answer_facts.Vision: To become a paragon institution for pursuance of Education and Research in Engineering and Technology
answer_facts.Mission: Impart finest quality Technical Education & Training, Nurture a vision of Sustainable development, Bequeath it to the next generation of professionals

answer_facts.Technical Clubs: IEEE Student Branch, CSI, ISTE, IETE, SAE
answer_facts.Cultural Clubs: Music club, Dance club, Drama club, Fine arts club
answer_facts.Other Clubs: Entrepreneurship cell, Literary club, Photography club, Nature club
answer_facts.Membership: Open to all students, nominal membership fee

answer_facts.Bus Service: College buses from major towns in Kasaragod district
answer_facts.Private Vehicles: Parking facility available for students and staff
answer_facts.Local Transport: Auto rickshaws and taxis available near campus
answer_facts.Safety: Female attendants in girls' buses, GPS tracking

answer_facts.Research: Active in research publications and funded projects
answer_facts.Departments: Separate departments for each engineering discipline
answer_facts.Development: Regular faculty development programs and workshops

answer_facts.Auditoriums: Main auditorium with seating capacity of 1500, plus open-air auditorium
answer_facts.Hostels: Separate hostels for boys and girls on campus
answer_facts.Other Facilities: Seminar halls, staff rooms, laboratories, college main stadium, multi-sports play space

answer_facts.Research Centers: Mechanical Engineering and Computer Science & Engineering recognized as research centres

answer_facts.PG Programs Added: Four post graduate programmes added during 2013-15

answer_facts.Faculty Research: Faculty involved in funded research projects and publications

answer_facts.IEDC Clubs: MULEARN, TINKERHUB, FOSS CLUB, CYBER COMMUNITY, MSA (Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors), CODERS CLUB, GALAXIA CLUB, GDG on Campus, YIP CLUB, WOMEN TECH MAKERS, KBA CHAPTER, WOW, AWS CLOUD CLUB
answer_facts.Technical Clubs: IEEE Student Branch, CSI, ISTE, IETE, SAE
answer_facts.Cultural Clubs: Music Club, Dance Club, Drama Club, Fine Arts Club
answer_facts.Other Clubs: Entrepreneurship Cell, Literary Club, Photography Club, Nature Club

answer_facts.Name: MULEARN
answer_facts.Description: Mulearn is an innovative learning platform that offers personalized educational experiences through adaptive technology, interactive content, and community collaboration. It enables learners to acquire new skills and knowledge at their own pace, with expert instructors and engaging content tailored to meet personal and professional development needs.

answer_facts.Name: TINKERHUB
answer_facts.Description: TinkerHub Foundation is a community of tinkerers, makers & students - working towards mapping and empowering people who share a passion to innovate.

answer_facts.Name: FOSS CLUB LBSCEK
answer_facts.Description: FOSS Club LBSCEK is a community of students passionate about Free and Open Source Software. We believe in learning, sharing, and contributing – the open way. Our club creates a space for collaboration, creativity, and innovation using open technologies.

answer_facts.Name: CYBER COMMUNITY
answer_facts.Description: Cyber Community at LBSCEK is a student-led learning and research group focused on practical cybersecurity, ethical hacking, and responsible use of technology. We bring together curious students from all departments to learn, build, and defend — not for show, but to gain real skills that matter in industry and research.
answer_facts.Activities: Hands-on workshops, expert talks, project-based learning, community awareness drives, and competitions
answer_facts.Focus: Learning-by-doing, building a sustainable student-run ecosystem where knowledge is shared, skills are practiced, and leadership is grown

answer_facts.Name: MSA - Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors
answer_facts.Description: The Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors (MLSA) program is a global initiative by Microsoft designed to empower students to become campus leaders in technology, innovation, and community building.
answer_facts.Benefits: Exclusive access to Microsoft resources, mentorship, and certifications
answer_facts.Technologies: Azure, Power Platform, GitHub, AI, and more
answer_facts.Skills Developed: Technical expertise, leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills through hosting events, mentoring peers, and contributing to real-world projects

answer_facts.Name: CODERS CLUB
answer_facts.Description: Coders Club is the hub for all things programming at LBS College of Engineering Kasaragod. We bring together students who are curious about coding, problem-solving, and building real-world projects.
answer_facts.Mission: Create a collaborative space where ideas turn into skills, and skills turn into innovation. We help beginners take their first steps in programming while also challenging advanced learners to push their limits.

answer_facts.Name: GALAXIA CLUB
answer_facts.Description: Galaxia is the official space club of LBS College of Engineering, Kasaragod. The club is dedicated to cultivating an interest in space science, astronomy, and space technology among students.
answer_facts.Focus: Fostering innovation, curiosity, and scientific temper by connecting students with experts and real-world applications of space tech
answer_facts.Activities: Broad spectrum of events and activities, building a vibrant campus community passionate about exploring the universe

answer_facts.Name: GDG (Google Developer Groups) on Campus LBSCEK
answer_facts.Description: GDG on Campus LBSCEK is a newly established student community at LBS College of Engineering, Kasaragod. The club brings together developers, designers, and tech enthusiasts who are eager to learn, build, and innovate with Google technologies.
answer_facts.Focus Areas: Web and mobile development, AI/ML, cloud computing, and design thinking
answer_facts.Goal: Inspire curiosity, foster innovation, and help students grow into future-ready developers and leaders through peer learning, workshops, and collaborations

answer_facts.Name: YIP CLUB
answer_facts.Description: YIP (Young Innovators Programme) is a key initiative of Kerala's K-DISC (Kerala Development and Innovation Strategic Council) that empowers students to create real-world solutions that drive sustainable and equitable development.

answer_facts.Name: WOMEN TECH MAKERS
answer_facts.Type: IEDC Club - Google Women in Tech Program
answer_facts.Description: Google's Women Techmakers (WTM) is a global program that provides visibility, community, and resources for women in technology, aiming to empower and encourage them to pursue and excel in tech careers.

answer_facts.Name: KBA CHAPTER - Kerala Blockchain Academy LBSCEK
answer_facts.Description: KBA LBSCEK is the official Kerala Blockchain Academy Chapter of LBS College of Engineering, Kasaragod. The chapter is committed to promoting awareness, learning, and innovation in blockchain technology among students.
answer_facts.Activities: Workshops, talks, and hands-on projects exploring blockchain in real-world applications
answer_facts.Focus: Nurturing technical expertise, entrepreneurial thinking, and innovation by bridging students with industry leaders and cutting-edge advancements in decentralized technologies

answer_facts.Name: WOW (Women of Wonder) Club
answer_facts.Description: The Women of Wonder (WOW) Club is a student-led initiative that empowers women through creativity, leadership, and social impact. It provides an inclusive space where students can voice their ideas, showcase talents, and inspire positive change.

answer_facts.Name: AWS CLOUD CLUB
answer_facts.Description: An AWS Cloud Club is a student-led community recognized by Amazon Web Services. It helps students learn cloud computing, build projects, and grow career skills with AWS.
answer_facts.Leadership: Each club is guided by a Faculty Advisor and a Campus Lead (student leader)
answer_facts.Focus: Cloud computing, AWS services, hands-on projects, and career development
